overall_justification: The edition features a prominent, multi-part article directly criticizing the expansionist arguments for the Caixa de Conversão, specifically targeting deputado David Campista's defense of a lower conversion rate.  The author meticulously dissects Campista's reasoning, highlighting its perceived flaws and warning of the dangers of inflation.  This detailed critique, combined with the absence of any counter-arguments or expansionist perspectives within the edition, strongly indicates a clearly orthodox editorial stance.

supporting_evidence.quote: A caixa impediria a alta; os apparelhos da lei de 1891 impossibilitariam a baixa, o cambio ficou sem limites.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote, from the beginning of the critical analysis, sets the stage for the orthodox argument by emphasizing the existing mechanisms (lei de 1891) already in place to prevent currency devaluation, implying that the Caixa is unnecessary.

supporting_evidence.quote: Se, porém, a caixa de conversão fixa o cambio, nenhum motivo haverá para que o ouro depositado emigre. Os bilhetes continuarão a circular, a “inflação” estará confirmada e o encarecimento geral da vida será a consequência inevitável da superabundância de moeda ou dos seus representativos.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote represents the core of the orthodox argument against the Caixa, warning of the inflationary consequences of a fixed exchange rate and increased money supply.

supporting_evidence.quote: Conseguintemente — superfluo ou não — o mecanismo da caixa — com o cambio fixo — é uma ameaça perpetua de inflacções crescentes, e chega-se a acreditar que para outro fim não foi ella inventada, e tão capciosamente defendida.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This concluding statement strongly reinforces the newspaper's orthodox position, portraying the Caixa as inherently inflationary and its proponents' arguments as deceptive.
